meta.note: Curated, sanitized projection of the internal platform roadmap (the canonical order+status doc) reconciled against milestone progress. Edit this file to evolve the public roadmap; the page renders from it. Public-site data honesty: no internal identifiers, hostnames, IPs, usernames, credentials, or codenames, and status reflects real progress — nothing marked shipped that isn't.

items.title: Move onto self-run baremetal
items.blurb: The work that organizes the quarter: migrating the platform off managed Kubernetes onto self-run Talos baremetal. Denser, cheaper capacity under our own control — measured by how fast it closes the dual-cluster window.

items.title: Isolated microVM dev environments
items.blurb: Per-engineer Firecracker microVMs with kernel-level isolation — live for the team today. A real machine each, not a shared namespace.

items.title: Single sign-on across the platform
items.blurb: Keycloak OIDC with Google Workspace brokering — one login for the registry, dashboards, GitOps, and internal tools, with group-based access. Live.

items.title: Self-hosted CI/CD with enforced review
items.blurb: Code review and merge move onto self-hosted Forgejo: required approvals, signed commits, and CI as real merge-blocking checks. GitHub stays a downstream mirror so releases and issue-linking keep working.

items.title: One keyless secret store
items.blurb: Every credential consolidates onto OpenBao behind OIDC-brokered auth — most of the way there. The goal is blunt: no secret ever copied by hand, no long-lived tokens sitting on disk.

items.title: Observability core
items.blurb: Prometheus, Grafana, Loki and tracing live across the cluster; service health is watched, not inferred. Extending the same coverage onto the baremetal substrate is the remaining piece.

items.title: Self-serve engineer onboarding
items.blurb: One flow from zero to operating the platform: access, identity, and a working environment, without a human in the loop for each step. Code-complete; enabling it broadly is next.

items.title: GitOps that tells the truth
items.blurb: Per-app deployment definitions and sync health gated on real apply state — so a green 'Healthy' can never hide a failed change underneath.

items.title: Dedicated GPU capacity
items.blurb: Bridge a rented-GPU spike to dedicated, colocated GPU for inference and training at scale — capacity we own rather than rent by the hour.

items.title: GPU inference & training serving
items.blurb: Model serving with scale-to-zero and fractional-GPU scheduling on the dedicated fleet — idle models cost nothing, hot ones burst.

items.title: Multi-tenant isolation, proven
items.blurb: Per-tenant namespaces and sandboxed execution proven out for safe shared use. A hard gate before anything general-availability builds on the platform.

items.title: Cost transparency
items.blurb: Real per-service cost surfaced inside the platform. Transparency about spend is identity here, not a feature.
